settings.title: The Stage Clients Never See
settings.excerpt: Before anything touches a screen, every project here starts as ink on cheap paper.
settings.body:
People assume design happens in software. At Pixelcraft, the real thinking happens earlier, in a stack of sketchbooks none of our clients will ever see.

We rough out twenty bad ideas by hand before we open the computer, because a pencil is faster than a mouse and it does not let you hide behind a nice gradient.

Why we start on paper

A blank screen tempts you to polish too early, to spend an hour making one weak idea look finished. Paper does the opposite. It is fast and unprecious, so we can throw an idea down, see that it is wrong, and move on without feeling attached to it.

  • Speed, since a rough sketch takes seconds.
  • Honesty, because a bad idea looks bad in pencil with nowhere to hide.
  • Volume, so the strong concept has plenty of competition.

Our designer Theo fills a notebook roughly every six weeks. By the time a concept reaches your inbox, it has already survived a few dozen rejected cousins.

What this means for your project

When we send you two or three directions, you are not seeing our first thoughts. You are seeing the ones that beat all the others on paper, which is why they tend to hold up.

It also means the early work moves quickly and cheaply. We do the messy exploration in a notebook, not on the clock in expensive software, and you get the benefit of all those discarded versions without paying for each one.
